sections.title: Product: T89/B Guide Rail (ISO 7465)
sections.content: The T89/B is the most common elevator guide rail profile globally, used for passenger elevators up to 1,600 kg capacity and speeds up to 2.5 m/s. This BOM decomposition traces the T89/B from finished component (L4) down to raw materials (L0). [SRC: web_search]

sections.title: BOM Hierarchy (L4 to L0)
sections.content:
| Level | Component | Material | Specification | Weight | Cost Share |
|-------|-----------|----------|--------------|--------|-----------|
| L4 | T89/B guide rail assembly (5m length) | - | ISO 7465, EN 81-50 | 42.65 kg | 100% |
| L3.1 | T89/B profile bar (5m) | C45+QT or 50CrMo4 | 89mm web, 62mm base, 16mm flange | 42.65 kg | 72% |
| L3.2 | Machined joint face (both ends) | Same as profile | Milled flat, drilled 2x M12 | incl. | 8% |
| L3.3 | Induction-hardened running surface | Same as profile | 50-55 HRC, depth 2-3mm | incl. | 12% |
| L3.4 | Surface treatment | Priming / oiling | Corrosion protection for transport | incl. | 3% |
| L3.5 | Markings / labeling | - | CE mark, batch number, EN 81 reference | incl. | <1% |
| L3.6 | Quality certificate | - | EN 10204 Type 3.1 inspection certificate | - | 5% |

sections.title: L3.1 Breakdown: Profile Bar Production
sections.content:
| Step | Input | Process | Output | Yield | ZOLLERN Capability |
|------|-------|---------|--------|-------|-------------------|
| L2.1 | Billet (150x150mm, C45) | Reheating to 1,150 C | Hot billet | 99% | YES — reheating furnace |
| L2.2 | Hot billet | Roughing passes (3-4) | Intermediate T-shape | 97% | YES — hot rolling mill |
| L2.3 | Intermediate shape | Finishing passes (2-3) | T89/B near-net cross-section | 96% | YES — finishing stands |
| L2.4 | Hot-rolled bar | Cooling bed + straightening | Straight bar, +/-0.5mm | 99% | YES — roller straightener |
| L2.5 | Straight bar | CNC cutting to 5,000mm +/-1mm | Cut-to-length bars | 98% (end crop) | YES — CNC saws |
| L2.6 | Cut bar | Induction hardening (rail head) | Hardened running surface | 100% | YES — induction line |

**Cumulative yield: ~89%** (billet to finished profile)

sections.title: Material Alternatives and Market Shares
sections.content:
| Material Grade | Standard | Tensile Strength | Application | Market Share | Price EUR/t |
|---------------|----------|-----------------|-------------|-------------|------------|
| C45+QT | EN 10083-2 | 560-710 MPa | Standard T-rails, residential | 45% | 850-950 |
| 50CrMo4+QT | EN 10083-3 | 900-1,100 MPa | High-speed elevators (>2.5 m/s) | 25% | 1,100-1,300 |
| S355J2 | EN 10025-2 | 470-630 MPa | Low-speed freight elevators | 20% | 750-850 |
| 16MnCr5 | EN 10084 | 590-880 MPa | Case-hardened rails (specialty) | 5% | 1,000-1,150 |
| 42CrMo4+QT | EN 10083-3 | 750-900 MPa | Heavy-duty, high-cycle | 5% | 1,050-1,200 |

sections.title: Manufacturing Cost Breakdown (per meter of T89/B)
sections.content:
| Cost Element | EUR/m | Share | ZOLLERN Advantage |
|-------------|-------|-------|-------------------|
| Raw material (billet) | 5.80 | 42% | NNS reduces material need by 35% vs machined |
| Hot rolling (energy + labor) | 2.90 | 21% | Core competency — existing capacity |
| Induction hardening | 1.40 | 10% | In-house capability, no subcontracting |
| Straightening + cutting | 0.85 | 6% | Automated line |
| Quality inspection + cert | 0.70 | 5% | EN 10204 Type 3.1 capability |
| Tooling amortization | 0.55 | 4% | EUR 15-20K die cost over 500t minimum |
| Overhead (factory, admin) | 1.60 | 12% | Fixed — improves with utilization |
| **Total manufacturing cost** | **13.80** | **100%** | |
| **Selling price (market)** | **15.50-18.00** | | **Gross margin 12-23%** |

sections.title: NNS Advantage vs. Machined Bar Stock
sections.content:
| Parameter | ZOLLERN NNS Profile | Machined from Flat Bar | Savings |
|-----------|--------------------|-----------------------|---------|
| Starting material weight (per m) | 9.50 kg | 14.60 kg | 35% material |
| Machining time | 0 min (as-rolled) | 12-18 min/m | 100% |
| Material cost / m | EUR 5.80 | EUR 8.90 | EUR 3.10/m (35%) |
| Machining cost / m | EUR 0 | EUR 4.50 | EUR 4.50/m |
| Total cost / m | EUR 13.80 | EUR 19.40 | EUR 5.60/m (29%) |
| Grain flow | Aligned to T-profile | Cut through grains | +20% fatigue life |
